acceptance_checks_for_revision: New calculations_and_validation.md present and matches CSV values and sums.
Verification script runs without error and reports total_cost == 3610.
conflicts.md contains inline numeric citations for totals and per-allocation numbers.
Metadata and grader/verifier outputs no longer contain contradictory semantic evaluation fields.
verification_script_examples.node_one_liner: const demand=[39,63,34].reduce((a,b)=>a+b,0); const cap=[58,82,53].reduce((a,b)=>a+b,0); const cost=53*23+58*27+25*33; console.log({total_demand:demand,total_capacity:cap,total_cost:cost});

planned_changes: Add calculations_and_validation.md containing: (a) explicit arithmetic lines reproducing demand and capacity totals with citations, (b) per-resource supply totals, (c) recomputed per-resource costs and the final total cost, and (d) sensitivity statements matching conflicts.md.

Add a small verification script (verify.js or verify.py) that reads ground_pass_schedule.csv and recomputes totals and costs, outputting pass/fail and the numeric breakdown. Include executable header and a one-line run instruction.

Update conflicts.md to include inline numeric citations adjacent to each critical numeric statement (totals and allocation quantities) and add a link/reference to calculations_and_validation.md and the verification script.

Update attempt metadata and any grader output templates to ensure deterministic_precheck metadata and semantic scoring fields are consistent (i.e., if deterministic precheck does not evaluate semantics, do not include semantic_score claims in raw model output).
Run the deterministic precheck and verifier locally (or via CI) to confirm no metadata inconsistencies remain and that the new verification script passes.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Close minor verification/usability gaps: provide machine-readable validation, make numeric citations explicit inline, and remove the internal reporting inconsistency so automated verifiers and humans can re-check the math effortlessly.
